A letter between friends

The Thread

April 2026
Written by
Caroline · Kyla · Sarah
This Month's Updates
Caroline
📍 Bend, Oregon
🏔️
✨ Highlight: Finally got that solo ski day and started following recipes — miracles do happen!

April finally started feeling like spring out here, which in Bend means everyone is immediately outside, on bikes, on trails, and in the Deschutes River — basically, any excuse to not be indoors. I finally snagged that solo ski day I'd been postponing all season, and let me tell you, it was worth every single 'I'll do it next weekend.'

Work has been a bizarre cocktail of busy and slow — like trying to sprint through molasses. There's a new project on the horizon that I'm cautiously excited about, but I'm trying not to jinx it by manifesting anything just yet. On a culinary note, I've started cooking more seriously. Yes, that means actually following recipes instead of my usual freestyle approach — which often ends with me wondering why dinner tastes like a mystery.

I miss you both terribly. I've been reminiscing about that week at the lake and really want to plan another getaway before summer slips through our fingers.

Kyla
📍 Columbus, Ohio
🌷
✨ Highlight: Wore a coat and shorts in the same day, and Gerald the pothos is still alive—big wins all around!

Columbus has been doing its classic 'four seasons in one week' thing again. I've managed to wear a coat and shorts within the same 48-hour window. Twice. It's like nature's little game of 'guess the weather,' and I'm just here for the ride.

Lately, I've been in a real reading phase—three books in three weeks, which feels like a personal record. I've mostly dived into fiction, which is refreshing after a long hiatus. I thought all that non-fiction was making me smarter, but it turns out it was just making me anxious about systems beyond my control. So, back to fiction, where the stakes are high but the consequences are blissfully imaginary.

Also, exciting news: Gerald the pothos is thriving! This feels monumental given my track record with plants. I've never successfully kept one alive before, so this feels like personal growth. Who knew I'd find a green thumb amidst the pages of a novel?

Sarah
📍 Cleveland, Ohio
⚽
✨ Highlight: Survived Dyngus Day's squirt gun antics and dived into house hunting in Brecksville!

April was a month of transitions and squirt gun battles, starting with what might be our last Dyngus Day celebration. It's hard to say goodbye to a day full of food, polka, and friends, but Vela made the most of it by transforming our backyard into a splash zone. I'll be sending some pictures your way—imagine pure joy and wet chaos.

In bigger news, we've started house hunting in Brecksville. The search is on, but in the meantime, we've decided to keep Vela with our beloved nanny, Sammi, until kindergarten. Why rush growing up when you can enjoy your scooter in the first warm weather of the year?

Work has been its usual mix of busy and engaging, with a sprinkle of upcoming change due to my boss getting a new boss. That should be interesting. I'm gearing up for our company conference, CommunityLive, which means more travel in May and June. With Jules and Dewey on parental leave, my workload has expanded, but who can resist the chaos?

Looking ahead, we're excited for summer projects on the house, weekend escapades with Vela and Chris, and getting the boat back in the water. May can't come soon enough!

A note from all three of us

As we navigate April's unpredictable weather and shifting rhythms, it seems we're all embracing a blend of change and continuity—Caroline's culinary adventures, Kyla's literary escapades, and Sarah's spirited family celebrations. Our lives are humming with new projects and personal growth, yet we find ourselves reminiscing and planning for shared moments. Here's to sprinting through molasses and finding joy in the chaos—may May bring us all a bit closer to sunlit days and another lakeside getaway.
